typecheck_backend.details: 95 errors in 13 files — pre-existing type errors scheduled for fix in milestone 'deployment' (feature deploy-mypy-fixes)

validatorNote: Backend mypy fails with 95 pre-existing type errors. These are not introduced by video-replay milestone and are explicitly scheduled for fix in milestone 'deployment' (feature deploy-mypy-fixes). All other validators pass cleanly.

blockingIssues.description: NovaActAuditProvider.run_audit() returns video_urls={} (hardcoded empty dict) instead of collecting video URLs extracted by individual scenario methods. Video URLs are saved to storage and events are emitted, but they are never propagated back via BrowserRunResult to the orchestrator. PlaywrightAuditProvider correctly returns video_urls. Fix: collect video URLs from scenario methods into a shared dict (thread-safe) and return in BrowserRunResult.

parseVideoUrls in ReportPage.tsx splits video_urls keys (format: '{scenario}_{persona}') using split(/[_-]/), which incorrectly splits multi-word names. For 'cookie_consent_privacy_sensitive', this produces scenario='cookie', persona='consent' instead of 'Cookie Consent'/'Privacy Sensitive'. Video card labels are wrong on ReportPage.

PersonaDiffPage video matching uses same flawed split(/[_-]/) parsing. parts[1]='consent' never matches persona names like 'privacy_sensitive', so video links NEVER appear on PersonaDiffPage. The VAL-VIDEO-007 requirement is non-functional.

ReportPage video tests pass misleadingly: getAllByText('Cookie Consent') matches hero pills from selected_scenarios, not video card labels. No scoped assertions verify video section content. No PersonaDiffPage video tests exist at all.

appliedUpdates.description: Created .factory/library/video-data-model.md documenting the video_urls key format convention ('{scenario}_{persona}' with underscore ambiguity), correct parsing approach (cross-reference with selected_scenarios/personas), video file format, storage key pattern, and data flow.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add testing convention: 'Tests must use tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() or pytest tmp_path for file storage, never hardcoded paths like ./data/test_storage'. This prevents leftover test artifacts on disk.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature video-backend-model-and-mock: backend/tests/test_video_recording.py lines 79, 100 use './data/test_storage' while lines 130, 289 correctly use tempfile.TemporaryDirectory().

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Emphasize that manual browser verification (Step 6) is MANDATORY even when all automated tests pass. UI rendering correctness (labels, layout, visibility) is often not caught by tests that match text from other page elements. Add note: 'If tests pass but you skipped manual verification, you may be masking bugs. Tests can match text from unrelated elements.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature video-frontend-player worker skipped manual verification because 'all automated tests pass', but the parsing bug showed incorrect labels. getAllByText matched hero pills, not video section.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a step for re-reviewing existing implementations: 'If the feature was already implemented by a prior worker session, do NOT assume it is correct. Review the existing implementation against the feature spec and validation assertions before limiting your changes to fixes.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature video-frontend-player worker found prior implementation (d71626fa) and only fixed TypeScript compilation errors without reviewing correctness. The prior implementation contained the parseVideoUrls parsing bug.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify logging convention: The codebase uses stdlib logging (not structlog) in providers (browser.py, nova_act_browser.py). AGENTS.md mentions structlog but providers use logging.getLogger(__name__). Standardize to one or clarify which modules use which.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature video-nova-act-integration added inline 'import logging' in browser.py:472 exception handler. nova_act_browser.py uses module-level logger = logging.getLogger(__name__). Neither uses structlog.

rejectedObservations.observation: Commit hygiene: unrelated file (verify_backfill.py) included in commit 4c1b6a5
rejectedObservations.reason: One-off mistake, not systemic. Adding commit hygiene guidelines to AGENTS.md would be disproportionate.

rejectedObservations.observation: Nova Act data flow pattern for collecting video URLs from threaded execution not documented in AGENTS.md
rejectedObservations.reason: This is the blocking bug itself (hardcoded empty dict), not a documentation gap. Once fixed, the pattern will be self-evident in code.

rejectedObservations.observation: Server log files (server.err, server.log) committed to repo — should be in .gitignore
rejectedObservations.reason: Outside scope of video-replay milestone. Should be addressed but not as a scrutiny observation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Handoff commitId recording error (7b3c743 instead of 71428c6) for video-mock-events-and-integration
rejectedObservations.reason: Metadata recording error, not actionable through code changes.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill procedure lacks guidance for 'verification-only' features where no production code is needed
rejectedObservations.reason: Edge case — the worker handled it pragmatically. Adding skill procedure for this rare case would add complexity for little benefit.
